finding: A cross-platform evidence packet must be assembled only from independently verified retained observation bytes. A verifier's boolean alone is insufficient unless its exact observation digest, commit, platform, and classification agree with the supplied artifact.
design_implication: Insert a fail-closed packet assembler between per-platform byte verification and the existing cross-platform classifier. Emit no observations when any binding fails.

verification.runtime: isolated Node test execution against byte-identical pre-commit files
cases: accept exact Linux and macOS bindings
reject verifier result bound to another commit

falsification_route: Supply a verifier result whose observation_sha256 does not equal the downloaded observation bytes, whose commit differs, whose runner_os differs, or whose classification is not retained_bytes_match_observation; the assembler must reject and emit an empty observations array.
limits: No hosted GitHub Actions run was observed in this slice.
No Linux or macOS retained workflow artifact was downloaded.
Packet acceptance does not prove runner identity, workflow authenticity, classifier correctness, or universal crash safety.

Fresh web search did not surface the intended official GitHub documentation; this slice therefore relies on the already established in-toto and SLSA primary specifications and repository-local evidence contracts.
